events.title: Festival International Nuits d'Afrique
events.description: World-music festival with free outdoor concerts plus ticketed shows in downtown venues.

events.title: Just For Laughs / Juste pour rire
events.description: Major comedy festival with English and French programming across downtown venues.
events.notes: Verify exact English-language shows before booking.

events.title: Chợ Đêm MTL — Vietnamese Night Market
events.description: Night market with street food, vendors, and performances — good for easy summer wandering.

events.title: Festival Haïti en Folie
events.description: Haitian music, food, art, and cultural programming, largely centred on Parc La Fontaine.

events.title: Fierté Montréal
events.description: Major Pride festival, very English-friendly; Community Days and the Pride Parade are the easiest entry points.

events.title: Otakuthon
events.description: Anime and gaming convention with cosplay, Artist Alley, and panels — a fun nerdy weekend afternoon even for casual fans.

events.title: MUTEK
events.description: Electronic music and digital-art festival with immersive audiovisual programming — good for an experimental night out.

events.title: PHI — Paola Pivi / Jakob Kudsk Steensen / Habitat Sonore
events.description: Immersive art and audio exhibitions at the PHI Centre in Old Montréal.

events.title: Pointe-à-Callière — Alley-oop: An Interactive Adventure
events.description: Playful interactive museum exhibit — a good Old Montréal weekend outing.

events.title: Montréal Complètement Cirque
events.description: Circus festival with indoor shows plus free outdoor programming — a good 'something different' summer night.
events.notes: Final days — ends July 12.

events.title: Cirque du Soleil — Montréal shows
events.description: Big-ticket special night out; the Montréal page currently lists ECHO as a Big Top show plus nearby programming like LUZIA.
events.notes: Re-check exact show dates, venue, and ticket prices before booking.

events.title: Fantasia International Film Festival
events.description: Genre film festival — cult movies, horror, sci-fi, fantasy, and weird premieres. A great indoor summer night.

events.title: OSHEAGA
events.description: Montréal's biggest music festival. Scan the lineup and consider a single day rather than the full weekend.

events.title: POP Montréal
events.description: Indie music, film, art, and talks in small Plateau/Mile End venues, plus the Puces POP market.
events.notes: Lineup still being announced — re-check closer to the dates.

events.title: UCI Road World Championships
events.description: Major international cycling championships with citywide atmosphere, road closures, and free spectating along the route.
events.notes: Re-check route and spectator info closer to September.

events.title: Piknic Électronik
events.description: Outdoor electronic music on summer Sundays — daytime/early evening, lower commitment than a full festival.
events.notes: Seasonal; re-check the weekly lineup.

events.title: Montreal Ultimate
events.description: Recreational and competitive ultimate frisbee leagues — social, mixed-gender, and welcoming to non-elite players.
events.notes: Check beginner/rec registration windows in spring, summer, and fall.

events.title: Jarry Park beach volleyball courts
events.description: City beach-volleyball courts — the best informal pickup option near the Plateau, easy by bike or Metro.
events.notes: Verified facility but not an organized league; check evenings/weekends or Meetup/Facebook groups for pickup.

events.title: Sports Montréal
events.description: Adult sports, fitness, swimming, tennis, and aquatics programs — structured but accessible city sports.
events.notes: Re-check session registration windows; adult offerings currently include handball, badminton, ultimate, kickboxing, and tennis.

events.title: UQAM Centre sportif — indoor volleyball
events.description: Central, transit-friendly sports centre with volleyball facilities — a plausible winter indoor option.
events.notes: Verify current public/non-student access, volleyball libre, leagues, and session registration.

events.title: CEPSUM (Université de Montréal) — indoor volleyball
events.description: University sports complex with volleyball gyms, reachable by transit/REM — likely best for structured indoor play if public programming exists.
events.notes: Verify current public adult volleyball schedule, drop-in, or court rental.

events.title: Loisirs Montréal (city recreation portal)
events.description: Official city portal for sports, pools, fitness, and cultural classes — the cheapest and broadest source for city-run activities.
events.notes: Search UX is clunky; re-check when new city sessions open.

events.title: Club Sportif MAA
events.description: Private downtown athletic club with gym, classes, squash, and a wellness/social-club feel.
events.notes: Check membership and trial pricing.

events.title: YMCAs of Québec
events.description: Fitness, aquatics, community programs, and adult classes — approachable and less intimidating than boutique gyms.
events.notes: Check branch-specific schedules.

events.title: Montreal Shamrocks GAC
events.description: Gaelic football, hurling, and Irish sports club — social, English-friendly, and a different kind of athletics.
events.notes: Seasonal/club-based; check beginner welcomes and training nights.

events.title: Bixi weeknight activity loop
events.description: A classic Montréal bike loop: Plateau to Mount Royal to Outremont/Mile End and back. No registration, cheap, flexible.
events.notes: Best May–October.

events.title: Parc Sir-Wilfrid-Laurier
events.description: Plateau-adjacent park with a swimming pool, sports fields, outdoor gym, badminton and ping-pong courts, and picnic space.
events.notes: Re-check pool schedule seasonally.

events.title: Parc Jeanne-Mance
events.description: Tennis courts, soccer fields, wading pool, winter skating, and direct Mount Royal access.
events.notes: Re-check facilities due to ongoing park work.

events.title: Parc La Fontaine
events.description: The default Plateau park: walks, picnics, the pond, outdoor theatre, and skating in winter — easy to pair with dinner nearby.
events.notes: Re-check seasonal programming.

events.title: Complexe sportif Claude-Robillard
events.description: Serious municipal sports infrastructure — pool, track, and fitness access for structured training.
events.notes: Re-check public swim and track access.

events.title: Randolph Pub Ludique
events.description: Board-game pub with a large game library and staff recommendations — a very good low-stakes night out for two.
events.notes: Check reservations and events.

events.title: La Récréation
events.description: Board-game café/bar for a casual social night without needing to organize a group.
events.notes: Check hours and the event calendar.

events.title: L'Abyss
events.description: Gaming pub and nerd-culture venue — more event-driven and social than a standard board-game café.
events.notes: Check the calendar.

events.title: Meetup — Montréal board game groups
events.description: Recurring board-game nights across the city — good for meeting Anglos and newcomers; quality varies, so start with one-off events.
events.notes: Re-check weekly.

events.title: Brutopia Brewpub — trivia night
events.description: Downtown brewpub and live-event bar — a strong English-friendly candidate for a recurring pub quiz.
events.notes: Confirm the current trivia night on the venue calendar or socials before going.

events.title: McKibbin's Irish Pub — trivia night
events.description: English-friendly Irish-pub chain — a reliable setting for a casual pub quiz.
events.notes: Verify the intended branch and current weeknight schedule.

events.title: Ye Olde Orchard Pub & Grill — pub quiz
events.description: English/Irish-style pub with multiple locations — a good west-end alternative to a downtown quiz night.
events.notes: Confirm exact branch and current trivia schedule.

events.title: Hurley's Irish Pub — pub quiz
events.description: Classic downtown Irish pub — very English-friendly with a traditional pub-night feel.
events.notes: Check events and socials before going.

events.title: Burgundy Lion — quiz night
events.description: Polished British-style pub — worth a deliberate trivia-plus-dinner night out.
events.notes: Verify the current event schedule before making plans.

events.title: A/Maze Montréal
events.description: Escape rooms with several Montréal locations and themes — a cooperative puzzle night that works with just two people.
events.notes: Check room difficulty, minimum players, and language options before booking.

events.title: Escaparium
events.description: Destination escape rooms with elaborate, theatrical productions — better as a planned weekend outing than a weeknight.
events.notes: Check location, room theme, and travel time.

events.title: Ezkapaz
events.description: Escape-game venue worth comparing against A/Maze on themes and difficulty.
events.notes: Check room details and English availability.

events.title: Sauve Qui Peut
events.description: Additional escape-room option — a useful backup when the most popular rooms are booked.
events.notes: Check language, horror intensity, and minimum players.

events.title: Immersia
events.description: Escape-room option to compare by theme, group size, and location.
events.notes: Check language, horror intensity, and minimum players.

events.title: Mount Royal loop
events.description: The best car-free weeknight hike substitute — forest paths, viewpoints, and cafés before or after. Easy to moderate depending on route.

events.title: Gault Nature Reserve / Mont Saint-Hilaire
events.description: A 25 km trail network over four summits above Lac Hertel — proper nature roughly a half-day trip by car. Moderate difficulty.
events.notes: Reserve/check passes and trail conditions before going.

events.title: Parc national du Mont-Saint-Bruno
events.description: Lakes and forest paths — an approachable hike-and-picnic day, easy to moderate.
events.notes: Check SEPAQ access and parking.

events.title: Parc national d'Oka
events.description: Beach plus hiking plus picnic — better as a summer day trip than a pure hike. Easy to moderate.
events.notes: Seasonal; check beach and parking conditions.

events.title: Parc national des Îles-de-Boucherville
events.description: Flat island trails with biking, kayaking, and picnic options — outdoors without a strenuous climb.

events.title: MMFA — Rising Suns: Art from the Confederacies of the Great Lakes and Rivers
events.description: Substantial exhibition of Indigenous art and history from the Great Lakes confederacies.

events.title: MMFA — Beyond the Pedestal
events.description: Longer-running exhibition — a useful backup for cold or rainy weekends.

events.title: MMFA — Chiharu Shiota: The Soul Trembles
events.description: Major immersive installation-art show opening in the fall — one to plan around.
events.notes: Upcoming — opens September 26.

events.title: Fireworks viewing — L'International des Feux
events.description: Free summer-evening fireworks viewing from the bridge or Old Port on competition nights — pairs well with a waterfront walk or Old Montréal dinner.
events.notes: Verify show dates before going.

events.title: Escher / optical-illusion exhibit (watch item)
events.location: TBD — PHI, OASIS, Arsenal, MMFA, or McCord
events.description: No confirmed Montréal Escher exhibition currently — tracked because impossible-geometry and optical-illusion shows are a strong fit if one appears.
events.notes: Unconfirmed — re-check venues before making plans.

events.title: McCord Stewart Museum — Céline in Dior: A Dazzling Moment
events.description: Approachable fashion and pop-culture exhibition.

events.title: McCord Stewart Museum — Montréal 1976: An Olympic Feat
events.description: Local history with a sports, design, and architecture angle on the 1976 Olympics.

events.title: McCord Stewart Museum — On the Menu Montreal: A Restaurant Story
events.description: Montréal restaurant and food history — a good pre-dinner museum visit.

events.title: MMFA — The Torlonia Collection: Masterpieces of Roman Sculpture
events.description: Major classical-sculpture exhibition in its final days.
events.notes: Closes July 19 — go soon if interested.

events.title: MMFA — Richard Avedon: Immortal. Portraits of Aging, 1951–2004
events.description: Photography exhibition — an easier lift than a full permanent-collection day.

events.title: MMFA — Keerat Kaur: Sweet Speech
events.description: Contemporary exhibition at the MMFA.

events.title: MMFA — Ready, Set, Go! Sports Inspire Art
events.description: Sports-meets-art exhibition, timely in a major cycling-championship year.

events.title: Pointe-à-Callière — Heroes of Greece: The Age of Troy
events.description: Long-running exhibition on Greek heroes and Troy — combine with dinner or a waterfront walk in Old Montréal.

events.title: Pointe-à-Callière — Montréal 1976: The Olympic Legacy
events.description: Olympic-legacy exhibition at the archaeology museum.
